title: Plan
body:
# Klar Digital — 7-Day Delivery Workflow: Sale to Launch

## Overview

A simple, repeatable, and scalable workflow to deliver a conversion-focused website in 7 business days. Every client engagement follows this exact sequence. No exceptions, no scope creep.

**Core principles:**
- Every day has one clear objective and one clear owner
- Client has exactly 2 touchpoints requiring their action (Day 1 intake, Day 5 review)
- Internal handoffs happen at end-of-day via a shared project board
- All templates and checklists are reusable across every project

---

## Day-by-Day Workflow

### Day 1 — Kickoff & Intake

**Owner:** Project Manager (PM)
**Objective:** Collect everything needed to build the site. Nothing more.

| Step | Action | Tool/Template |
|------|--------|---------------|
| 1 | Send welcome email with intake form link | Welcome Email Template |
| 2 | 30-min kickoff call (video or phone) | Kickoff Script |
| 3 | Collect: logo, brand colors, photos, copy preferences, competitor examples | Intake Checklist |
| 4 | Confirm package scope (Starter / Growth / Premium) | Package Scope Card |
| 5 | PM fills out Project Brief and posts to project board | Project Brief Template |

**Deliverable:** Completed Project Brief on the project board, ready for design.

**Intake Checklist:**
- [ ] Business name and contact info
- [ ] Logo files (or approval to create simple text logo)
- [ ] Brand colors (or "use your judgment")
- [ ] 5-10 photos (or approval to use stock)
- [ ] Key services / products list
- [ ] Desired call-to-action (book, call, visit, buy)
- [ ] Competitor/inspiration URLs (up to 3)
- [ ] Domain access or domain to purchase
- [ ] Hosting preference (we recommend managed hosting)

---

### Day 2 — Design & Structure

**Owner:** Designer
**Objective:** Create the visual direction and page layout.

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Review Project Brief |
| 2 | Select site template from Klar template library |
| 3 | Customize colors, fonts, imagery to match client brand |
| 4 | Create wireframe/mockup for homepage + key pages |
| 5 | Post design preview to project board for internal review |

**Deliverable:** Design mockup (homepage + 1 inner page) posted for internal QA.

**Design Standards:**
- Mobile-first responsive layout
- Max 3 font weights, 2-3 brand colors + neutrals
- Above-the-fold hero with clear CTA
- Social proof section (reviews, logos, stats)
- Fast-loading (target < 2s load time)

---

### Day 3 — Content & Copy

**Owner:** Copywriter / PM (depending on package)
**Objective:** Write all site copy, finalized and ready for dev.

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Write homepage copy (hero, services, about, CTA) |
| 2 | Write inner page copy (About, Services, Contact) |
| 3 | Write meta titles and descriptions for SEO |
| 4 | Source/select final images |
| 5 | All copy added to shared doc, linked in project board |

**Deliverable:** Final copy document with all text and image selections.

**Copy Framework (per page):**
- Headline: Pain point or aspiration (max 10 words)
- Subheadline: What we do and for whom (max 20 words)
- Body: 3-4 short paragraphs, benefits over features
- CTA: One clear action per section

---

### Day 4 — Development / Build

**Owner:** Developer
**Objective:** Build the live site from approved design + copy.

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Set up hosting and domain (if not done) |
| 2 | Build pages using approved template + design |
| 3 | Insert all copy and images |
| 4 | Set up contact form / booking integration |
| 5 | Mobile responsiveness check |
| 6 | Basic SEO setup (meta tags, sitemap, robots.txt) |

**Deliverable:** Fully built site on staging URL.

**Dev Checklist:**
- [ ] All pages built and linked
- [ ] Contact form working (test submission received)
- [ ] Images optimized (WebP, lazy loading)
- [ ] Mobile responsive (test on 3 screen sizes)
- [ ] Favicon and OG image set
- [ ] Google Analytics / tracking pixel installed
- [ ] SSL certificate active
- [ ] Page speed score > 90 (Lighthouse)

---

### Day 5 — Client Review

**Owner:** PM
**Objective:** Get client approval or collect revision requests (max 2 rounds).

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Send staging link to client with review guide |
| 2 | 15-min walkthrough call (optional but recommended) |
| 3 | Client submits feedback via feedback form |
| 4 | PM categorizes feedback: in-scope vs out-of-scope |
| 5 | PM posts revision list to project board |

**Deliverable:** Approved revision list (or full approval to launch).

**Client Review Guide (sent to client):**
- Review on both phone and desktop
- Check all text for accuracy
- Test the contact form
- Confirm call-to-action is correct
- Note up to 5 changes (included in package)
- Revisions beyond scope are quoted separately

---

### Day 6 — Revisions

**Owner:** Developer + Designer (as needed)
**Objective:** Implement all approved revisions.

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Implement text/image changes |
| 2 | Implement layout/design adjustments |
| 3 | Re-run QA checklist (Dev Checklist from Day 4) |
| 4 | Send updated staging link to client for final sign-off |
| 5 | Client confirms approval (email or form) |

**Deliverable:** Client-approved final site, ready for launch.

**Revision Rules:**
- Max 2 revision rounds included in all packages
- Each round: up to 5 changes
- Structural changes (new pages, new features) are out-of-scope
- Additional revisions billed at hourly rate

---

### Day 7 — Launch & Handoff

**Owner:** Developer (launch) + PM (handoff)
**Objective:** Go live and hand over to client.

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Point domain to live hosting |
| 2 | Final cross-browser and mobile check |
| 3 | Submit to Google Search Console |
| 4 | Send launch email to client with handoff package |
| 5 | Post-launch 24-hour monitoring (uptime, forms, speed) |
| 6 | Schedule Day 14 follow-up call |

**Deliverable:** Live website + Handoff Package sent to client.

**Handoff Package includes:**
- Login credentials (hosting, CMS if applicable)
- Quick-start guide for making basic edits
- Support contact info
- Monthly maintenance plan details (if purchased)
- Invoice for any add-ons

---

## Project Management Templates

### 1. Project Board Structure (per client)

| Column | Cards |
|--------|-------|
| **Intake** | Project Brief, Intake Checklist |
| **Design** | Design Mockup, Brand Assets |
| **Content** | Copy Doc, Image Library |
| **Build** | Dev Checklist, Staging URL |
| **Review** | Client Feedback, Revision List |
| **Launch** | Launch Checklist, Handoff Package |
| **Done** | Archived deliverables |

### 2. Handoff Matrix

| From | To | When | What is handed off |
|------|----|------|--------------------|
| Sales | PM | Day 0 (sale closed) | Client contact, package selected, payment confirmed |
| PM | Designer | End of Day 1 | Completed Project Brief + all assets |
| Designer | Copywriter | End of Day 2 | Approved design mockup + template selection |
| Copywriter | Developer | End of Day 3 | Final copy doc + images |
| Developer | PM | End of Day 4 | Staging URL + completed dev checklist |
| PM | Client | Day 5 | Staging link + review guide |
| Client | PM | Day 5 | Feedback / approval |
| PM | Developer | Day 5-6 | Approved revision list |
| Developer | PM | End of Day 6 | Updated staging URL |
| PM + Dev | Client | Day 7 | Live site + handoff package |

### 3. Status Communication Template

**Daily internal update (posted to project board):**
```
Client: [Name]
Day: [X/7]
Status: On Track / At Risk / Blocked
Today completed: [what]
Tomorrow: [what]
Blockers: [none / description]
```

**Client-facing updates (sent Day 1, Day 5, Day 7 only):**
- Day 1: Welcome + what to expect
- Day 5: Review request
- Day 7: Launch announcement + handoff

---

## Scalability Notes

- This workflow supports **one PM managing 3-4 concurrent projects** (staggered start dates)
- Designer and Developer can each handle **2-3 concurrent projects** at different stages
- For higher volume: add a second designer or developer, keep PM as bottleneck controller
- All templates are reusable — new projects start from copy, not scratch
- Target: 4-6 websites delivered per month with a 3-person team (PM + Designer + Dev)
